title: hypericum quadrangulum subsp. immaculatum
intro: Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. immaculatum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family, characterized by its four-angled stems and white or pale yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin compounds. The medicinal properties of Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. immaculatum are said to offer health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and antiviral activity, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
